english: Jimin and I have come to a disagreement. As the new Minister of Security after Zal's arrest, he's developed an acute sense of...self-importance. He feels that his word rivals even that of the emperor.Midway through our excavation of the swamp, we encountered a naga nest. Rather than leave them be, Jimin (who I refuse to dub "Minister" in my personal records) ordered his standing army to take the land anyway. I believe he fancies it the site of his new ministry headquarters, given the surveyor's full report on the surrounding land. It would land in the dead center of the new city, which is "ideal" to some...but is it worth displacing a species from their home?I do not believe so, but I seem to be in the minority. Haebaragi, Emperor Bitgaram's daughter, seems to agree with my efforts.Minister Doh Taelin, Ministry of Archives, 1730 CC

Kaijun knelt before the bodies. The ritual was complete; everyone that had once done her harm met a potent, venomous death. She placed the knife on the ground and closed her eyes. For the first time since the incident so many years ago, she smiled. She smiled for the life she'd once lived, the souls she once aided. But that woman was gone, snuffed out by years of torture and cruelty.When she arose from the floor, she was swallowed by the stars. She took her place in the land of the Celestials quickly. Silently.

Karetia, or whoever finds this—I'm sorry.    The clouds are getting darker by the hour. The air is filled with smoke and screams. After Jalios fell...that fear of death I'd snubbed came back with reprisal. I am afraid.    I know I'm needed here, but if this is really the end, do I want to die in Amnytas? Do I want to die knowing I'll never see my mother again? The dog? I've no need for frivolous attachments, but their faces sit in the back of my mind as I stare down these beasts from Nayos. If we really are to die—if they are, too—then my choice is me.    The ward has been my home and my family. Karetia, one of my great loves... The terms on which I surrender are not favorable, but they are my truth. And, once again, I'm sorry that this is what war has decided.     Ivane

Kasmeer Meade,  I expect that this letter will find you in respectable company and good health. Word has reached the Collective of your family's recent hardships, and we want to assure you that the status of your membership is unchanged. While I am not versed in the intricacies of human scandal, concerns were raised regarding your ability to perform tasks for us while also meeting your financial obligations.   Your sponsor has since stifled any concerns on your behalf, vouching for your strength of character, skill, and most importantly, discretion. If it would bolster your spirits, know that you have allies in your corner, including one with great influence.  In the meantime, should any situations arise, we will keep you in mind; your sponsor mentioned you were looking for work around Divinity's Reach. You may contact us if a reference is required. You know the way.  Regards,  Fourth Veil, Bengt Tovasson
